mechanism_narrative: Sustained month-over-month increases in nonfarm payrolls (PAYEMS) signal a strengthening labor market and improving aggregate demand. This leads to upward revisions in corporate earnings expectations, driving positive equity returns over the medium term.
failure_condition: The signal's rolling 3-year rank information coefficient (IC) versus 60-day forward returns falls below 0.03, or the hit rate of positive returns following positive payroll changes is below 55%.

mechanism_narrative: When the unemployment rate (UNRATE) is elevated above a long-term natural rate, it signals persistent economic slack and depressed consumer confidence. This negatively impacts forward earnings expectations and equity returns until the rate normalizes.
evidence_refs: fact:Current UNRATE of 8.4 is significantly above historical full-employment levels.
failure_condition: S&P 500 20-day forward returns are not statistically lower on average during periods when UNRATE > 6.0% compared to periods when it is below the threshold (t-test p > 0.05).

mechanism_narrative: A rapid increase in the Consumer Price Index (CPIAUCSL) signals rising inflation, which increases the probability of future monetary policy tightening. This expectation raises the discount rate applied to future corporate cash flows, compressing equity valuations.
failure_condition: Periods of accelerating inflation (trigger met) are not followed by negative S&P 500 returns over the subsequent 60 trading days more than 50% of the time.

mechanism_narrative: A simultaneous decline in industrial production (INDPRO) and a rise in inflation (CPIAUCSL) creates a stagflationary environment. This combination erodes corporate profit margins and dampens investor sentiment, leading to negative equity returns.
failure_condition: The dual-trigger condition does not lead to statistically significant negative returns compared to a baseline where neither or only one condition is met.

mechanism_narrative: In a post-shock recovery regime with high unemployment, an extremely low federal funds rate (FEDFUNDS) provides a powerful tailwind for equities by ensuring cheap credit and suppressing the risk-free rate, boosting valuations via multiple expansion.
evidence_refs: fact:Current context is ZLB policy with high unemployment.
failure_condition: The positive effect on returns disappears or reverses when unemployment falls below 6.5%, even if the Fed funds rate remains low, indicating the mechanism is regime-dependent.

mechanism_narrative: Strong month-over-month growth in retail sales (RSAFS) is a direct indicator of robust consumer demand. This translates into higher near-term corporate revenues, supporting positive short-term equity market performance around the data release.
failure_condition: A positive surprise in the monthly RSAFS release is not followed by positive S&P 500 returns over the next 5 trading days more than 55% of the time over a 5-year sample.

mechanism_narrative: An acceleration in housing starts (HOUST) serves as a leading indicator for broader economic activity, as it signals household formation and drives demand in numerous related sectors. This forward-looking strength supports equity prices over a medium-term horizon.
candidate_trigger: 3-month moving average of HOUST is rising
evidence_refs: fact:Current HOUST value of 1496 is historically strong.
failure_condition: The correlation between the 3-month change in HOUST and 60-day forward S&P 500 returns is not statistically positive (p > 0.05) over any 5-year rolling window.

mechanism_narrative: Positive quarter-over-quarter growth in real GDP (GDPC1) confirms broad economic expansion, validating optimistic earnings forecasts and reducing perceived recession risk, which drives equity markets higher following the release.
failure_condition: Positive S&P 500 returns do not follow positive quarterly GDPC1 releases in a statistically significant manner (e.g., hit rate of positive 20d returns is less than 60%).
